steps.title: Substitute the repeated expression
steps.content:
The expression $(8 - 2x)$ appears in both terms on the left and right sides of the equation. Rather than expanding and distributing, substitute $u = 8 - 2x$ to simplify:

$$5u + 3 = 4u + 15$$

steps.title: Solve the linear equation in u
steps.content:
Subtract $4u$ and subtract \$3$ from both sides:

$$5u - 4u = 15 - 3$$

$$u = 12$$

Since $u = 8 - 2x$, the value of $8 - 2x$ is \$12$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
